Eucalyptus ferruginea, also known as the rusty gum or ironbark, is a species of tree in the myrtle family, native to Australia.

The health benefits of Eucalyptus ferruginea include its potential to help with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. It also has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.

The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus ferruginea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Eucalyptus ferruginea include tea, essential oil, and capsules or tablets, which can be used to relieve respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.

uses: Etubila ferruginea has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with applications in treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
